steps.title: What is an x-intercept?
steps.content: An x-intercept occurs where the graph crosses the x-axis, meaning y = 0. We need to find the point(s) where the parabola meets the horizontal line y = 0.

steps.title: Reading the graph: one touching point
steps.content: The parabola is a narrow upward-opening curve whose vertex sits directly on the x-axis at (4, 0). The curve is tangent to the x-axis at this single point — it touches but does not cross. There is exactly one x-intercept at x = 4.

steps.title: Identifying the correct choice
steps.content:
Among the given choices, (4, 0) is the point where the parabola touches the x-axis.

Checking alternatives: choices A and C involve negative x-values not shown on the visible domain; choice B, (5, 0), is wrong because x = 5 lies on the rising right branch of the parabola, which is above the x-axis at that point — the curve does not pass through (5, 0).

The point (4, 0) matches choice D — D is correct.
